question.text: Under Section 15-I, what must the Adjudicating Officer consider while imposing penalties?

answer.text: Whether the person made profits or averted losses, whether there was repetition, and whether the penalty is proportionate to the default
Only the amount of profit made by the violator
Only whether the violation was intentional or accidental
Only the size of the intermediary and its annual turnover

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The factors include: amount of disproportionate gain or unfair advantage, amount of loss caused to investors, repetitive nature of the default, and whether the penalty is proportionate and commensurate with the nature and gravity of the default. **The Trap:** Multiple factors are considered, not just profit or intent alone. The proportionality principle ensures penalties match the severity of the violation. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Repeat offenders face higher scrutiny — the repetitive nature of default is a specific factor, making first-time cooperation more favorable.
